heading: Monitoring compliance of manufacturers
label: 33108 Monitoring compliance of manufacturers
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)33108(a)15:2026(a)., , , § 606; added , , .Oct. 20, 1972Oct. 25, 1984Pub. L. 92–51386 Stat. 947Pub. children.text: allow an officer or employee designated by the Secretary to inspect the vehicles and parts and relevant records of the manufacturer.

children.heading: .—Entry and Inspection
children.label: b .—Entry and Inspection
children.text: To enforce this chapter, an officer or employee designated by the Secretary, on presenting appropriate credentials and a written notice to the owner, operator, or agent in charge, may inspect a facility in which motor vehicles containing major parts subject to the standard, or major replacement parts subject to the standard, are manufactured, held for introduction into interstate commerce, or held for sale after introduction into interstate commerce. An inspection shall be conducted at a reasonable time, in a reasonable way, and with reasonable promptness.

A manufacturer of a motor vehicle subject to the standard, and a manufacturer of a major replacement part subject to the standard, shall provide at the time of delivery of the vehicle or part a certification that the vehicle or part conforms to the applicable motor vehicle theft prevention standard. The certification shall accompany the vehicle or part until its delivery to the first purchaser. The Secretary by regulation may prescribe the type and form of the certification.

children.label: B
children.text: labeled only for export on the vehicle or replacement part and the outside of any container until exported; and

there is an error in the identification (required by the standard) applied to a major part installed by the manufacturer in a motor vehicle during its assembly, or to a major replacement part manufactured by the manufacturer; and
the motor vehicle or major replacement part has entered interstate commerce.
